jokes.text: 'Cause of the way you get them.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks why they wanted kids after commenting how nice it is to have the house to themselves
jokes.punchline: Philip's suggestive response about the conception process
jokes.explanation: Solid innuendo with good economy. Philip's character allows for this married couple humor.

jokes.text: Who that say they gonna beat Will Smith? What, what?
jokes.setup: Will enters hyped up about their victory
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated street bravado and physical gestures
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith swagger entrance. Good character integration but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Why don't you go upstairs, get my wallet... and take your friends to the movies? In Pittsburgh.
jokes.setup: Philip seemingly offers to treat Will's friends to movies
jokes.punchline: The escalation to sending them to another state entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. Philip's deadpan delivery of wanting the kids gone far away is well-crafted.

jokes.text: That's very honorable, son. You're grounded.
jokes.setup: Carlton takes responsibility for bringing the team over unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Philip's fake praise followed by immediate punishment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Philip's parental authority. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Actually, we got kicked out of six other people's cribs and a 7-Eleven parking lot.
jokes.setup: Vivian thinks it's nice the kids want to be around them
jokes.punchline: Will reveals they're there by elimination, not choice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character honesty. The 7-Eleven detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Hey, but this is now, and that was this morning.
jokes.setup: Will reminisces about past conflicts with Philip
jokes.punchline: The 'past' was actually just hours ago
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Great character moment showing Will's optimistic reframing.

jokes.text: No, I'm saving that for prom night.
jokes.setup: Tyson asks if Will has ever tried going to seven fast food joints on a city bus
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response about his romantic plans
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback but fairly predictable sarcasm. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: Then I should probably cancel my date with that fat, chocolate woman.
jokes.setup: Carlton warns about no fats, no chocolate, and no women while training
jokes.punchline: Will combines all three prohibitions into one imaginary date
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of Carlton's three prohibitions. Good structural economy.

jokes.text: Will, you pitiful Maalox poster child.
jokes.setup: Will eating unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Carlton's creative insult about Will's digestive future
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's very Carlton-like in its specificity and health consciousness.

jokes.text: Besides, I'm the greatest, I'm a bad man, and I'm pretty.
jokes.setup: Will boasting about basketball skills
jokes.punchline: Muhammad Ali impression/reference
jokes.explanation: Muhammad Ali reference fits Will's character but lacks originality. Crowd-pleasing but predictable.

jokes.text: My manager? I don't even want you to be my cousin.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling himself Will's manager
jokes.punchline: Will escalates from rejecting management to rejecting family relation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the cousin dynamic. Well-structured putdown.

jokes.text: He say he don't even want you to be his cousin.
jokes.setup: Will insults Carlton
jokes.punchline: Tyson unnecessarily repeats the insult to make sure Carlton heard
jokes.explanation: Supporting character adding insult to injury. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'll tell Dad that little circle in your wallet's not your lucky half-dollar.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatens to blackmail Will
jokes.punchline: Reveals Will carries condoms while pretending they're coins
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Carlton's knowledge of Will's deception and teenage concerns.

jokes.text: Shall we take my car or the Volvo?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking which car to take
jokes.punchline: Both options are his cars, showing his privilege
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment showing his oblivious privilege and multiple car ownership.

jokes.text: I'll say one thing for Malibu, the babes are primo-delecto.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on Malibu girls
jokes.punchline: Will's made-up Spanish-ish word 'primo-delecto'
jokes.explanation: Will's tendency to create pseudo-sophisticated language. Mildly amusing but not standout.

jokes.text: That must be jam, 'cause jelly don't shake like that.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on attractive women
jokes.punchline: Classic pickup line about body movement
jokes.explanation: Old pickup line. Gets a reaction but lacks originality.

jokes.text: We can go to my place and let freedom ring.
jokes.setup: Will offering to help with African American studies
jokes.punchline: Patriotic language used as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Clever use of patriotic language for pickup purposes. Moderately clever but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The only tough break he had is when the Izod shop ran out of pink.
jokes.setup: Will dismissing Marcus as just a privileged Malibu kid
jokes.punchline: Stereotype about preppy fashion problems
jokes.explanation: Good stereotype joke about preppy problems. Well-constructed with good economy.

jokes.text: God, I hate when that happens.
jokes.setup: Will's joke about Izod shop running out of pink
jokes.punchline: Carlton sincerely relates to the preppy problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - he actually relates to the preppy problem Will was mocking.

jokes.text: Shut up, Smith. Can't you see I'm incognito?
jokes.setup: Coach trying to scout secretly
jokes.punchline: His 'disguise' is obviously ineffective
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy of coach's failed attempt at stealth scouting.

jokes.text: Not a damn thing, Will, it's part of the disguise.
jokes.setup: Will asks what the coach sees
jokes.punchline: Coach admits he can't see anything but claims it's intentional
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic. Coach's commitment to his failed disguise is perfectly illogical.

jokes.text: They said that I'm a superbly well-oiled machine... with the face of an angel and the body of a god.
jokes.setup: Will introducing himself to Marcus
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top self-description
jokes.explanation: Classic Will bravado. Good character integration but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Maybe that's not an exact quote.
jokes.setup: Will's over-the-top self-description
jokes.punchline: Understated admission that he embellished
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment where Will acknowledges his exaggeration. Good economy.

jokes.text: But I do recognize them ears. They covered up half of 'Dear Abby.'
jokes.setup: Marcus saying he doesn't recognize Will's face
jokes.punchline: Insult about Will's large ears blocking newspaper text
jokes.explanation: Great comeback with specific visual imagery. Creative insult with good structure.

jokes.text: I'm glad you're familiar with that... because my butt is the only part of my anatomy you gonna see next week.
jokes.setup: Marcus says he'll do the same thing to Will's butt
jokes.punchline: Will turns it around suggesting he'll be ahead of Marcus
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback in the trash talk sequence. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Only scout looking for you is trying to sell you some cookies.
jokes.setup: Will mentions the scout coming for him
jokes.punchline: Marcus suggests it's a Girl Scout selling cookies
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'scout' - Girl Scout vs sports scout. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: Most Likely to Bag my Groceries.
jokes.setup: Marcus congratulating Will ahead of time
jokes.punchline: For a service job rather than athletic achievement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from athletic achievement to menial job. Well-structured putdown.

jokes.text: You may be tall and good-looking and a good basketball player... but you're not that nice.
jokes.setup: Will building up to a comeback
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic criticism about personality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Sets up for a devastating insult but delivers mild personality critique. Great subversion.

jokes.text: I'm sure they do, they can check their makeup in it.
jokes.setup: Comment about girls liking the bald barber's head
jokes.punchline: Because it's so shiny it works as a mirror
jokes.explanation: Classic bald joke with good economy. Competent but predictable.

jokes.text: In other words, for a small fee, I might be persuaded to date your daughters.
jokes.setup: Will boasting about his basketball performance
jokes.punchline: Offering romantic services as if they're privileges
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's ego. The 'small fee' addition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We ain't had no sneakers, so we played in high-top combat boots. And we ain't had no basketball, so we used a brick.
jokes.setup: Old man talking about tough childhood basketball
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd substitutions for equipment
jokes.explanation: Great escalating 'tough childhood' story. Good character work from the old-timer.

jokes.text: Good thing you all wasn't into archery.
jokes.setup: Old man's story about hitting cousin in the head with bricks
jokes.punchline: Will imagines how much worse archery would have been
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with dark humor. Perfect economy and great logical extension of the story.

jokes.text: Just because the roof blew off don't mean there ain't a log on the fire.
jokes.setup: Response to joke about his baldness
jokes.punchline: Folksy metaphor about still having game despite being bald
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific comeback with colorful folk wisdom. Very authentic to the character.

jokes.text: Wait, I thought you lived out in Malibu. I know you thought I did.
jokes.setup: Will's assumption about Marcus being from Malibu
jokes.punchline: Marcus reveals he let Will think that intentionally
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist moment. Marcus's strategic deception adds depth to his character.

jokes.text: Nope. I run.
jokes.setup: Question about riding the bus 20 miles to school
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of running 40 miles daily
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an incredible feat. Excellent economy - two words carry huge impact.

jokes.text: Don't cut me too short, though. I don't want you give me no 'Hair Jordan.'
jokes.setup: Will asking for a haircut before the big game
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'Air Jordan' with fear of baldness
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on Air Jordan. Good wordplay that fits the basketball context.

jokes.text: Our motto is: 'It may be nappy, but we'll make it snappy.'
jokes.setup: Nelson promoting his barbershop
jokes.punchline: Rhyming motto about hair texture and service speed
jokes.explanation: Good rhyming slogan that works for the character and setting. Culturally specific humor.

jokes.text: He used to play on the asphalt in his socks. Mother couldn't afford sneakers, you know.
jokes.setup: Nelson talking about young Marcus's toughness
jokes.punchline: Revealing the economic hardship behind the toughness
jokes.explanation: Poignant detail that adds depth to Marcus's character

jokes.text: I was the only kid in preschool who knew how to downshift a big rig.
jokes.setup: Will talking about having multiple jobs as a kid
jokes.punchline: Absurd specific detail about trucking knowledge in preschool
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist detail. The specificity of 'downshift a big rig' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A few years from now I'll be dunking baskets in the NBA... Marcus will be dunking donuts in a deep fryer.
jokes.setup: Will predicting his NBA future
jokes.punchline: Parallel structure contrasting his success with Marcus's failure
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure with good wordplay on 'dunking'

jokes.text: 'Junior'? Yeah, Will, this is my son. My son.
jokes.setup: Will calls the kid 'Junior' dismissively
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the kid is Marcus, Will's rival's father
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist moment. Great misdirection that recontextualizes the entire barbershop scene.

jokes.text: Dr. Ruth, and Dr. Pepper.
jokes.setup: Will listing his dream team including doctors
jokes.punchline: Including a sex therapist and a soft drink as 'doctors'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on 'doctor.' Gets a chuckle but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: And another thing, take this $1,000 bill and wipe your baby's nose.
jokes.setup: Will in his success fantasy
jokes.punchline: Using expensive money for mundane baby care
jokes.explanation: Dream logic showing Will's fantasized wealth

jokes.text: Hello, Dada?
jokes.setup: Will denying paternity in his dream
jokes.punchline: Baby immediately calls him 'Dada'
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom baby joke. Perfect timing but predictable premise.

jokes.text: I dreamed you had hair like Don King. Did I look taller?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about Will's dream
jokes.punchline: Carlton's vanity about height overshadows hair concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his height insecurity trumps all other concerns.

jokes.text: You just hand out cigars and hope its head rounds out.
jokes.setup: Will asking if having a kid is rough
jokes.punchline: Carlton's naive understanding of fatherhood
jokes.explanation: Great Carlton innocence. The 'head rounds out' detail is perfectly naive.

jokes.text: That, and pass it to Will.
jokes.setup: Coach giving pre-game pep talk about coming a long way
jokes.punchline: Simple basketball strategy as the only real advice
jokes.explanation: Good coaching cliché subversion. Economy is excellent with just the essential words.

jokes.text: Sorry, it's a bad analogy.
jokes.setup: Coach uses 'stick to him like white on rice' to Will
jokes.punchline: Immediate acknowledgment of the racial awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment addressing racial implications of common phrase. Great meta-humor.

jokes.text: My situation does not define who I am, I define who I am.
jokes.setup: Marcus rejecting Will's charity assumption
jokes.punchline: Serious character moment that's also unexpectedly profound
jokes.explanation: More dramatic than comedic, but the earnestness in a comedy context creates humor through tonal shift.

jokes.text: All due respect, my brother... I don't think nobody's gonna offer you no million dollars.
jokes.setup: Marcus talking about million dollar offers
jokes.punchline: Will's brutally honest assessment delivered gently
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's honesty wrapped in politeness. Solid construction.

jokes.text: All due respect, my brother... I don't think nobody's gonna offer you no million dollars.
jokes.setup: Will talking about million-dollar basketball offers
jokes.punchline: Marcus's polite but cutting assessment of Will's prospects
jokes.explanation: Polite but brutal reality check delivered with respect

jokes.text: Smith has the ball. He dribbles, he drives between the legs. He stops, he pops. And it's good! The crowd goes wild. Oh, Smith hurt his ankle. He hurt his ankle. It's over, he cannot play. Smith wins, 1-0.
jokes.setup: Will and Marcus playing one-on-one
jokes.punchline: Will narrating his own game and declaring victory after getting injured
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy with Will's delusional victory declaration. Perfect character moment.
